content.text:
*shifts, looks away*

I mean. She was... difficult. Demanding. My whole life I was the one who managed her. My brother got to just be the kid, and I was the one she called when things fell apart. Every time. For thirty years.

*pause*

And then at the end I wasn't there. Which is — I don't know what that means about me.

*quieter*

There's some relief in there too. That it's over. That she can't call anymore. And that makes me feel like a terrible person so I don't really think about it.

*beat, then back to deflection*

But you didn't answer my question. About the body. Doesn't it know what it needs? A bottle of wine a night — maybe that's just what's required right now. To get through it.
